mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >60% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal shows no predictive power in periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when productivity declines, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real retail sales validates top-down economic growth with bottom-up consumer confirmation, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases when government spending drives GDP growth, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruption, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of stable inventory levels.
